text:
## Overview

Ran is a 1985 epic samurai film directed by Akira Kurosawa. It is a jidaigeki depiction of the fall of a powerful warlord and his three sons. The film transposes the plot of Shakespeare's King Lear to Sengoku-era Japan.

## Production

Kurosawa spent nearly a decade planning Ran before securing French and Japanese financing. At the time of its release it was the most expensive Japanese film ever produced. The screenplay was co-written by Kurosawa, Hideo Oguni, and Masato Ide.

## Early life

Akira Kurosawa was a Japanese filmmaker and painter who directed thirty films across a career spanning five decades. He was born in Tokyo in 1910. He is widely regarded as one of the most important and influential filmmakers in the history of cinema.

## Recognition

In 1990 Kurosawa accepted an Academy Honorary Award for cinematic accomplishments. His films, including Seven Samurai, Rashomon, and Ran, profoundly influenced directors around the world.

## Description

The Kintai Bridge is a historical wooden arch bridge in the city of Iwakuni, in Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan. It spans the Nishiki River in a series of five wooden arches. The bridge was built in 1673 and has been reconstructed several times since.

## Overview

Seven Samurai is a 1954 Japanese epic samurai drama film co-written, edited, and directed by Akira Kurosawa. The story follows a village of farmers that hire seven masterless samurai to combat bandits who will return after the harvest to steal their crops.

## Sparse retrieval

BM25 is a bag-of-words ranking function that scores documents by term frequency and inverse document frequency, normalized for document length. It catches exact keyword and entity matches that dense embeddings can miss.

## Dense retrieval

A bi-encoder embeds the query and documents into a shared vector space so that semantically similar texts land near each other. Reciprocal Rank Fusion combines a sparse and a dense ranked list into a single ordering.
